sb_prompt: FIND OUT WHAT BOOKS THE OTHER CALLER READS FOR ENJOYMENT OR SELF-IMPROVEMENT.         DO YOU HAVE SIMILAR OR DIFFERENT INTERESTS IN BOOKS?
turns: I love to read, what about you?
Oh, I'm a big reader too. What kind of books do you like to read?
I usually read mysteries and thrillers.
I enjoy those too, but I also like to read non-fiction.
I haven't read much non-fiction lately, but I used to love biographies.
I've been reading a lot of history lately, especially about the World War II era.
Have you read any good ones lately?
I just finished a great one about Eleanor Roosevelt.
Oh, I've been wanting to read that one.
What's your favorite history book about World War II?
I recently read "The Boys in the Boat" and found it really engaging.
Have you read any other books about that time period?
I haven't read any about World War II, but I've heard good things about "Unbroken" by Laura Hillenbrand.
I've actually read that one too, it's a great read.
I might have to give that one a try.
Yeah, it's definitely worth picking up.
So, what kind of non-fiction books do you usually read?
I usually stick to biographies of people I admire, like Nelson Mandela or Malala Yousafzai.
I really admire those people too.
I also love reading about inspirational figures.
Have you read any books about female leaders or activists?
Yes, I recently read "The Handmaid's Tale" by Margaret Atwood.
What did you think of "The Handmaid's Tale"?
I found it to be a really powerful and thought-provoking book.
Oh, I've heard of that one, but I haven't gotten around to reading it yet.
Have you read any other books by Margaret Atwood?
Yes, I've also read "Oryx and Crake".
I've been wanting to read that one too.
Yeah, it's definitely worth picking up.
Have you read any other dystopian novels?
Yes, I've also read "The Hunger Games" trilogy.
What did you think of "The Hunger Games"?
I thought it was a really gripping read.
What's your favorite book by Margaret Atwood?
I think my favorite book by her is actually 'The Blind Assassin'.
I've heard that one's really good too.
So, have you read any other books by Canadian authors?
